knowledge_components.name: Negative numbers are values less than zero and are the additive opposites to positive numbers

complicators.name: Recognize and represent when negative integers are applicable
Recognize the additive inverse for rational numbers

knowledge_components.name: Understanding negative numbers on the number line involves both position (for comparing and ordering) and distance from zero (absolute value)

complicators.name: Represent negative rational values in a model
Understand absolute value as distance from zero on the number line and evaluate absolute value expressions with concrete numbers.
Compare and order two rational numbers

knowledge_components.name: Adding and subtracting integers uses the additive inverse principle to combine opposite quantities

complicators.name: Add two integers where the sum is not zero
Recognize subtraction as adding the additive inverse: a - b = a + (-b)
Add and subtract multi-step expressions with negative and positive values

knowledge_components.name: Operations with negative integers follow consistent patterns: multiplication and division have sign rules while absolute value and negation expressions must be carefully parsed using order of operations

complicators.name: Apply sign rules for multiplication and division with two factors
Apply sign rules for multiplication and division for 3-4 terms
Simplify expressions with negative bases, paying attention to parentheses
Parse and evaluate complex absolute value and negation notation: -|x| vs -|-x| vs -(-x)

knowledge_components.name: Arithmetic properties maintain consistency when extended to additive inverses and integer operations

complicators.name: Apply commutative and associative properties with positive and negative integers
Use distributive property with negative numbers
Simplify two-step expressions with negative integers, following the order of operations convention
Simplify multi-operation expressions with negative values, following the order of operations convention
